html:
The Electrical Doodad is a Tinkering Tab Item exclusive to Wagstaff. It costs 1 Cut Stone and 2 Gold Nuggets to craft. It can be used to craft the Infroggles, the Telebrella and the Telipad.

In DLCs and Don't Starve Together, the Electrical Doodad can be crafted by all Characters. It is found in the Science Tab and requires a Science Machine to prototype. It is used in several more recipes, such as the Alchemy Engine, Endothermic Fire Pit, and Ice Flingomatic.

In Reign of Giants DLC, Electrical Doodads have a small chance to be coughed up by friendly Catcoons.

In Shipwrecked DLC, Electrical Doodads also have a small chance to be found in X Marks the Spot treasure chests.

Trivia

  • Electrical Doodads were first introduced in the Reign of Giants DLC. During the Early Access phase of the Hamlet DLC, they were then retroactively added to the base game to allow Wagstaff to craft his specific items.
  • Electrical Doodads used to be crafted from the Refine Tab.
  • Electrical Doodads were initially added to make it harder to craft crucial objects, like the Alchemy Engine.
  • Per Wagstaff's quote as well as its appearance, the Electrical Doodad is a diode despite its spawn code referring to it as a transistor.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

The Electrical Doodad is a Tinkering Tab Item exclusive to Wagstaff. It costs 1 Cut Stone and 2 Gold Nuggets to craft. It can be used to craft the Infroggles, the Telebrella and the Telipad.
In DLCs and Don't Starve Together, the Electrical Doodad can be crafted by all Characters. It is found in the Science Tab and requires a Science Machine to prototype. It is used in several more recipes, such as the Alchemy Engine, Endothermic Fire Pit, and Ice Flingomatic.
In Reign of Giants DLC, Electrical Doodads have a small chance to be coughed up by friendly Catcoons.
In Shipwrecked DLC, Electrical Doodads also have a small chance to be found in X Marks the Spot treasure chests.

Trivia[edit | edit source]
Electrical Doodads were first introduced in the Reign of Giants DLC. During the Early Access phase of the Hamlet DLC, they were then retroactively added to the base game to allow Wagstaff to craft his specific items.
Electrical Doodads used to be crafted from the Refine Tab.
Electrical Doodads were initially added to make it harder to craft crucial objects, like the Alchemy Engine.
Per Wagstaff's quote as well as its appearance, the Electrical Doodad is a diode despite its spawn code referring to it as a transistor.

The '''Electrical Doodad''' is a [[Tinkering Tab]] [[Item]] exclusive to [[Wagstaff]]. It costs 1 [[Cut Stone]] and 2 [[Gold Nugget]]s to craft. It can be used to craft the [[Infroggles]], the [[Telebrella]] and the [[Telipad]].

In [[DLC]]s and ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', the Electrical Doodad can be crafted by all [[Characters]]. It is found in the [[Science Tab]] and requires a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. It is used in several more recipes, such as the [[Alchemy Engine]], [[Endothermic Fire#Endothermic Fire Pit|Endothermic Fire Pit]], and [[Ice Flingomatic]].

In ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, Electrical Doodads have a small chance to be coughed up by friendly [[Catcoon]]s.

In ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, Electrical Doodads also have a small chance to be found in [[X Marks the Spot]] treasure chests.

{{Clear}}

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Electrical Doodads were first introduced in the ''Reign of Giants'' DLC. During the Early Access phase of the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, they were then retroactively added to the base game to allow Wagstaff to craft his specific items.
* Electrical Doodads used to be crafted from the [[Refine Tab]].
* Electrical Doodads were initially added to make it harder to craft crucial objects, like the Alchemy Engine.
* Per Wagstaff's quote as well as its appearance, the Electrical Doodad is a diode despite its spawn code referring to it as a transistor.
